description: Reconcile repository-facing contributor docs and planner-facing factory docs with the merged Phase 1–6 state—README Contents taxonomy, review policy automation accuracy, cross-doc category consistency, and factory overview retargeting—so Phase 7 content seeding starts from one coherent source of truth without adding list entries.

context.customerAsk: Reconcile repo-facing and planner-facing docs with the now-merged Phase 1-6 state so Phase 7 content seeding starts from one coherent source of truth. Update README.md Contents to reflect actual top-level list sections including Related Lists and stop implying Community is a curated category. Update docs/review-policy.md for current automation via make targets, Go checks, and GitHub workflows while preserving manual review for scope, quality, and convergence. Keep README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/review-policy.md, and docs/taxonomy.md consistent on ten curated categories and contributor submission path. Update factory/docs/overview.md for awesome-agent-factories and remove nonexistent file references. Leave make check, go test ./..., and git diff --check passing. Do not add Phase 7 content entries.
context.problem: After Phase 6 template convergence, documentation drift remains: README Contents omits Related Lists but lists Community as navigation; docs/review-policy.md still claims Phase 4 automation does not exist despite make check, Go checks, and GitHub workflows on main; docs/taxonomy.md says Phase 4 is unimplemented and its quick-reference table omits Related Lists; factory/docs/overview.md still describes an AI model reference website and references missing paths such as docs/documentation-site-pages-needed.md. Contributors and factory executors therefore read conflicting guidance before Phase 7 content seeding.
context.solution: Branch phase-6-7-docs-convergence-cleanup from main and reconcile README Contents plus minimal checker alignment, modernize review-policy automation framing, align taxonomy and contributing cross-references on ten categories, rewrite factory/docs/overview.md for this repository, and verify make check, go test ./..., and git diff --check pass without adding Phase 7 list content.

acceptanceCriteria: README.md Contents navigation lists all ten curated category sections including Related Lists and does not present Community as a curated content category

docs/review-policy.md accurately describes automated enforcement via make targets, Go README checks, and GitHub workflows while stating maintainers still perform manual review for scope, quality, category fit, and convergence decisions
README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/review-policy.md, and docs/taxonomy.md agree on the ten curated categories and contributor submission path without contradictory automation claims
factory/docs/overview.md describes Awesome AI Agent Factories and references only existing repository paths and factory CLI entry points
No Phase 7 README resource entries or content seeding changes are introduced in this batch
Changed files stay within documentation reconciliation scope plus minimal internal/checks updates required for make check after Contents alignment
Quality gate: make check, go test ./..., and git diff --check all pass from the repository root on the reconciliation branch

userStories.title: Align README Contents with curated taxonomy
userStories.description: As a reader browsing the list, I want the Contents navigation to reflect the ten curated resource sections—including Related Lists—so I can jump to any category without mistaking the Community footer for a list category.
acceptanceCriteria: README ## Contents links to all ten curated category headings: Theories, Coordination Patterns, Frameworks, Protocols and Interfaces, Benchmarks, Research Papers, Blog Posts, Case Studies, Examples and Templates, and Related Lists
README ## Contents includes Related Lists and does not include Community as a curated navigation item; the ## Community section may remain at the document footer for conduct and security links
README ## Contributing prose states contributors choose among the ten curated categories and does not describe Community as a submission category
make check passes after Contents changes, including minimal internal/checks updates only if required so Related Lists is validated in Contents
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Modernize review policy for automation plus manual review
userStories.description: As a maintainer or contributor reading review policy, I want an accurate description of what automation enforces today versus what still requires human judgment, so pre-submit self-checks and maintainer review align with repository reality.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/review-policy.md opening documents automated checks via local make targets (make check, make test, make links), Go README validation in internal/checks, and GitHub workflows (ci.yml, link-check.yml, awesome-lint.yml, scheduled-maintenance.yml), and states maintainers still perform manual review for scope fit, quality, category disputes, and convergence decisions
Checklist item 8 (link stability) references automated link checking via make links and the Link Check workflow instead of claiming link checking is not enabled
Triage guidance for needs-link-review references automated link-check results where applicable
The Future automation (Phase 4) section is reframed so Phase 4 is described as implemented and future work covers checks not yet automated, without claiming automation is absent
Recommended resource:* labels include Related Lists (for example resource:related-list) alongside the other nine category labels
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Reconcile taxonomy and contributor docs on ten categories
userStories.description: As a contributor choosing a README section, I want taxonomy, contributing, review policy, and README prose to name the same ten categories and submission path so I do not get conflicting guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/taxonomy.md category quick-reference table includes all ten README sections, including Related Lists, with a matching resource:* label entry
docs/taxonomy.md future-automation section states Phase 4 automated README checks and related CI are implemented and Phase 7 content seeding has not started in this batch

CONTRIBUTING.md category list, PR template pointers, and local-checks / GitHub Actions subsections remain aligned with README.md section headings and docs/review-policy.md without claiming Go checks or GitHub Actions are unconfigured
docs/review-policy.md checklist section 2 lists the same ten categories as CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/taxonomy.md, including Related Lists
No new README list entries or Phase 7 seed content are added
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Rewrite factory overview for this repository
userStories.description: As a factory operator or executor reading planner docs, I want factory/docs/overview.md to describe the Awesome AI Agent Factories repository and factory workflow so batch submission guidance points at real files and commands.
acceptanceCriteria: factory/docs/overview.md describes coordinating work for Awesome AI Agent Factories, not an AI model reference website or documentation site product

Read First references only paths that exist in this repository (for example factory/factory.json, factory/workstations/ideafy/AGENTS.md, factory/docs/batch-inputs.md, factory/docs/batch-input-example.json, and you docs agents / you docs batch-inputs CLI outputs)
docs/documentation-site-pages-needed.md and other nonexistent documentation-site paths are removed or replaced with valid awesome-agent-factories references
Phase control, work types, workstation flow, batch submission (you submit batch), state inspection, and repair guidance remain accurate for the checked-in factory/factory.json flow
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Verify doc reconciliation quality gates
userStories.description: As a maintainer preparing the Phase 6–7 convergence review, I want automated quality gates to pass and whitespace checks to stay clean so the reconciliation branch is safe to merge before Phase 7 content seeding.
acceptanceCriteria: From repository root, make check exits 0
From repository root, go test ./... exits 0
git diff --check reports no whitespace errors on changed files

Changed files are limited to documentation reconciliation scope: README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md only if needed for consistency, docs/review-policy.md, docs/taxonomy.md, factory/docs/overview.md, and minimal internal/checks updates required by README Contents alignment
No Phase 7 README resource entries are added
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
